title: Tirumala temple records highest-ever pilgrim footfalls and ‘hundi’ collection in June
content: More than 25 lakh devotees visit the hill shrine and offer a staggering ₹126 crore during the month, which also witnesses a record distribution of 1.19 crore laddus

title: Bombay High Court upholds helmet exemption for Sikhs as reasonable classification
content: The court dismissed a law student’s petition challenging the exemption, ruling that it does not violate Article 14 of the Constitution

title: ‘Jailer 2’: Rajinikanth-Nelson film gets release date
content: Starring the iconic Rajinikanth and directed by Nelson, the sequel to the 2023 blockbuster will have music from Anirudh Ravichander

title: As Visakhapatnam rises skyward, groundwater sinks in Yendada-Madhurawada corridor
content: Experts warn that the city’s rapid vertical growth is outpacing water supply and natural recharge, leaving its fastest-growing suburbs more and more dependent on borewells and tankers

title: Naidu, Pawan, Chouhan launch VB-G Ram G scheme in Tirupati district
content: Launched at Mukkavaripalli in Tirupati district, the renewed rural employment scheme puts farmer welfare first; the Centre also offered to procure 2.16 lakh tonnes of Totapuri mangoes

title: Mumbai man dies falling into manhole, third rain-related incident
content: The man, identified as 55-year-old Aslam Esaf Shaikh, was taken to the Rajawadi Hospital in Ghatkopar, where the doctors declared him brought dead

title: Influencer Joseph ‘Prasna Raavan’ held in Hyderabad for remarks against Pawan Kalyan
content: Joseph Bachalakuri, arrested in Hyderabad on a Jana Sena leader’s complaint, will be produced before a Pithapuram court; a case has been booked under the IT Act

title: The Hindu Group’s Chairman, Director call on Tamil Nadu CM Vijay
content: Chairman N. Murali congratulated the Chief Minister on the TVK’s historic victory in the 2026 Assembly elections

title: Andhra Pradesh raises retirement age to 62 for PSU, Corporation employees
content: The decision extends to employees of these organisations the retirement age already made applicable to State government employees under the Andhra Pradesh Public Employment (Regulation of Age of Superannuation) (Amendment) Act, 2022

title: Twenty women bound for Muscat detained at Hyderabad airport with fake visas
content: The women told officials that a travel agent had arranged the documents after promising them jobs in Muscat and they were unaware the visas were fake

title: Can Bar Associations refuse to represent an accused? What does the law say?
content: The Faizabad Bar Association’s resolution to not represent the accused in the Ayodhya Ram Temple embezzlement case has prompted the question on whether a Bar Association could collectively decide not to represent a particular accused

title: The guides Hyderabad stopped looking for
content: A tour of Hyderabad’s heritage structures started with hiring a guide. Guides used to be the first thing visitors looked for, not an afterthought. Digital tools have disrupted this lot as well. Now, the profession has become a side gig for many. Yet, experienced guides still wait to walk people telling stories of the structures.

title: CM Fadnavis orders stay on new RTI rules after Hazare's agitation threat
content: Anna Hazare had threatened to launch an indefinite hunger strike from July 5 if the amendments to the RTI rules were not withdrawn

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Man dies after falling into manhole amid heavy rains in Mumbai
content: The incident occurred in the Chandivali area, where waterlogging apparently concealed the uncovered manhole

title: Madras High Court refuses to quash case against Ponmudy for 2025 derogatory speech
content: Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan dismisses a criminal revision petition filed by the former Minister against the complaint having been taken cognisance of by a metropolitan magistrate

title: Congress questions BJP’s silence over alleged land deals of M.P. CM Mohan Yadav in Ujjain, seeks probe
content: It has been alleged that Mohan Yadav’s family and associates were involved in large scale land purchases in Ujjain’s areas where development projects were planned

title: Rabri Devi moves to own house, gives up government bungalow she had occupied for two decades
content: The development is being seen as indicative of a diminution in clout of the proverbial first family of the Opposition party, which was on good terms with the ruling dispensation until it was headed by JD(U) president

title: Socioeconomic status has an impact on premature mortality, shows Chennai study
content: The study indicates that reducing premature mortality in India will take more than just creating deterrence for smoking and drinking alcohol

title: Death of Mayiladuthurai teen duo: Post-mortem done on body of Scheduled Caste youth, girl’s father arrested
content: The youth had previously alleged in the police complaint that he was abused and assaulted by the father of the girl and a few others.

title: Watch: A theft at a temple: Implications of the Ayodhya embezzlement case
content: After years of anticipation, with India’s apex court getting involved, the Ayodhya Ram Mandir was consecrated in January 2024, under The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust. But more than two years on, the same trust is embroiled in a controversy, that crores of donations to the temple by pilgrims, have been embezzled. We look at what happened and the repurcussions of that event.

title: Congress-BRS confrontation escalates into showdown in Hyderabad; alleged scam at Telangana’s Gurukuls under spotlight
content: Leaders challenged each other to public debates over allegations of irregularities in the procurement of uniforms, shoes and school bags for students of Telangana’s residential educational institutions (Gurkulams)

title: Seven killed as boulder collapses at stone quarry on outskirts of Bengaluru, death toll likely to rise
content: Police said several labourers were reported missing, raising fears that the death toll could increase

title: BJP will get neither donations, nor votes, says Akhilesh Yadav
content: Invoking Lord Ram and the Constitution, Akhilesh Yadav claimed the BJP had betrayed both religious values and democratic principles

title: Rules governing crèches in India | Explained
content: The heart-wrenching incident of caregivers abusing toddlers at the ‘Little Buds Day Care’ on the Capgemini campus has sparked outrage and raised serious questions about the safety measures needed in childcare facilities

title: Keep me away from all this, I'm an artist: Diljit on CJP protests at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar
content: Everything in this world can never be right... So, those who are protesting, congratulations to them, and for whom the protest is happening, congratulations to them also, Diljit said

title: Police take Siya Goyal to Pune spot where she allegedly rehearsed fiancé’s murder
content: The police recovered the clothes allegedly worn by Siya on June 18, the day of the incident, during a search at her residence in the Market Yard area of Pune

title: Doorstep marijuana network using Speed Post busted, mastermind held after Hyderabad police intercept parcel
content: The network allegedly processed between 80 and 100 customer orders every day, dispatching eight to 10 Speed Post consignments daily. Each order was priced between ₹1,500 and ₹8,000, generating an estimated ₹1 lakh in daily earnings and an annual turnover of ₹4 crore to ₹5 crore.

title: Sabarimala gold loss: Former TDB president P.S. Prasanth denies role
content: Prasanth says ‘the only alleged lapse by certain officials’ was failing to inform Kerala High Court before sending gold-clad plates to Chennai for replating works in 2025

title: Supreme Court says AI-generated hallucinated precedents ‘catastrophic’ to judicial process, sets aside NCLT order
content: The top court also directed the Bar Council of India to constitute a committee to examine the issue and formulate appropriate norms

title: K.C. Venugopal writes to PM Modi, seeks Supreme Court-monitored probe into the Ram Mandir donation row
content: K.C. Venugopal questioned the credibility of the Special Investigation Team (SIT) constituted by the Uttar Pradesh government, saying it appeared to be little more than an “eyewash”

title: Amazon vows action against its delivery partner after Uttarakhand fire probe concludes
content: A fire at M&M Logistics Solutions, an Amazon delivery partner in Uttarakhand, killed two people on June 5 and the building ‌allegedly lacked valid fire safety clearance, alarms, smoke detectors, and proper emergency exits

title: Kerala HC refuses to extend transit bail granted to actor-husband of viral Kumbh Mela woman in kidnapping case
content: HC was informed by the State of Madhya Pradesh that anticipatory bail plea moved by Farmaan Khan had been dismissed by a Special Judge under POCSO Act in Mandleshwar, Madhya Pradesh

title: Karnataka CM raising Mekedatu dam issue for political gain: Anbumani Ramadoss
content: Demanding all MPs and MLAs to meet the Prime Minister and Jal Shakti Minister opposing the Mekedatu project, he said that continuous pressure should be given and many cases should be filed against this project.

title: India, Japan unveil transformative agenda to boost ties
content: The Prime Minister said India and Japan have also taken several important decisions in the field of energy security

title: Kerala teacher suspended after assault on Class II student in Kannur; police register case
content: Teacher allegedly hit the student on the back with his hand for being slow in copying notes from the blackboard

title: Pinarayi Vijayan alleges Finance Bill passed in violation of Assembly rules to benefit private liquor firms
content: Opposition Leader accuses Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan of showing ‘unnecessary haste and stubbornness to provide tax cuts’ to liquor companies

title: Kerala High Court asks Law Secretary and Cabinet Secretary to appear unless progress is made on draft anti-ragging amendment Bill
content: Court observes that the matter has been pending for more than a year.  Upon its request, Election Commission of India had approved a Cabinet meeting to finalise draft Kerala Prohibition of Ragging (Amendment) Bill during Kerala Assembly elections. After formation of new State government, three weeks were given to take steps on draft Bill on June 10

title: Foreign medical graduates allowed to take up internship in 36 non-teaching hospitals in Tamil Nadu
content: This has fulfilled a long-pending demand of hundreds of FMGs, who had passed the Foreign Medical Graduate Examination and were waiting for the mandatory CRMI

title: Tiruvallur ammonia gas leak: Death toll rises to 17
content: Of the 83 persons who were affected in the ammonia gas leak that occurred on June 21, 15 women are still hospitalised — one on ventilator support.

title: Contract of 134 Kerala’s Kudumbashree staff terminated
content: Order extending contract of other employees till September 30 issued. However, the staff under DAY-NULM project have not got any such relief

title: Woman stripped, paraded with shoe garland by partner’s family in Ujjain; 3 arrested
content: A 30-year-old woman faced brutal public humiliation in Ujjain's Jharda area after resuming a live-in relationship with a man recently released from jail. Her clothes were torn, face blackened, hair cut, and she was paraded with a shoe garland by her partner's father, relatives, and villagers. Police have registered a case against 11 accused and arrested three, citing anger over her return after a prior rape case.

title: 'You've to earn': Ex-India cricketer says Sooryavanshi shouldn't replace Samson
content: Sooryavanshi has travelled with the Indian squad for the T20I series against Ireland and England but is yet to make his international debut. India have instead continued to back the top-order trio of Sanju Samson, Abhishek Sharma and Ishan Kishan, all of whom played important roles in India's 2026 T20 World Cup triumph.

title: ‘Baaten Hain, Baaton Ka Kya’: Why India-Pakistan Track II, III, IV... diplomacy is a song on loop
content: A recent open letter from prominent Indian and Pakistani citizens urging renewed dialogue for peace faces an uphill battle. The article argues that India-Pakistan relations are stalled not by a lack of meetings, but by deep-seated ideological, political, and institutional divides. Pakistan's military establishment holds the real power, rendering civilian-led talks potentially futile.

title: Palantir CEO to companies using AI in 9-point note: Do not hand your data to LLM firms
content: Palantir has issued a stern warning to corporations regarding AI, urging them to retain proprietary data and avoid outsourcing critical operations to commercial LLM providers. The company's nine-point manifesto emphasizes "AI sovereignty," cautioning against "tokenmaxxing" – excessive spending on AI processing without a clear strategy. Palantir argues that controlling data and model "weights" is crucial for institutional future and competitive advantage, advising against decisions driven by politics or favoritism.

title: Australian cricketer faces jail after pleading guilty to child sex abuse charges
content: Former Australian cricketer Aaron Summers faces a possible jail sentence after pleading guilty to child sexual abuse and grooming offences committed in 2018 while he was with the Hobart Hurricanes. Summers, who previously served prison time for separate child exploitation offences, was remanded in custody after a Tasmanian court rejected the possibility of a suspended sentence. He will be sentenced in August.

title: Market wrap: Top gainers and losers on NSE Nifty50 and BSE Sensex today
content: Indian stock markets surged on Thursday, with Sensex and Nifty climbing nearly 1%. A significant rally in IT stocks, coupled with easing crude oil prices and positive India-Japan strategic agreements, boosted investor confidence. Despite some blue-chip weakness, the market extended its upward trend, driven by technology shares and optimism surrounding new economic and defence pacts.

title: After Meta layoffs, Zuckerberg says firms should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could create more jobs, a view contrasting with some industry leaders.

title: The 'surveillance gap' that hid months of Ram Temple donation 'theft'
content: Arrested accused Avinash Shukla has revealed a meticulous plan to steal Ram Temple donations. The gang studied CCTV blind spots, used human shields during counting, and smuggled cash out in small batches. A key to the counting room was held by an individual with no official role, further compromising security. Recoveries include over Rs 79 lakh in cash and valuables, highlighting significant lapses in the donation handling process.

title: Shreyas Iyer scripts history, becomes first Indian captain to...
content: Shreyas Iyer made history in Durham, becoming the first Indian captain to score a T20I half-century on English soil with a brilliant 68. His composed innings, crucial after an early collapse, helped India post 189/7. Though rain forced an abandonment, Iyer's return to form and record-breaking knock provides a significant boost for India ahead of the next match.

title: US pilot 'killed', plane torched in Papua; rebels call attack a 'message' to America & Indonesia
content: Separatist rebels in Papua claim to have killed an American pilot and burned a civilian plane, alleging it ferried Indonesian military personnel. The West Papua National Liberation Army stated the attack was a message to the US and Indonesian governments. Authorities confirmed the aircraft was found burnt but could not immediately verify the rebels' claims or the pilot's death. This incident escalates the ongoing low-level insurgency in the region.

title: Did Ukraine just use its first home-grown ballistic missile in combat? Russia makes big claim
content: Russia claims Ukraine may have launched its first indigenous ballistic missile, a potential game-changer in Kyiv's long-range strike capabilities. While Ukraine remains silent, the alleged interception comes as the Fire Point FP-9 missile nears flight testing. This development, if confirmed, signifies a significant escalation in Ukraine's efforts to develop its own advanced missile arsenal, capable of striking deep into Russian territory.

title: Cost, convenience, and validity: Why 11-month agreements are a norm in India
content: Rental agreements in India commonly last 11 months to bypass mandatory registration for longer terms, as per the Registration Act, 1908. This avoids additional stamp duty and registration fees, making the process more cost-effective and convenient for both landlords and tenants. Even unregistered, these agreements are legally valid if on stamp paper, offering a crucial record of tenancy terms.

title: PM Modi, 'younger sister' Takaichi sign MoC including $10 billion investment, AI boost
content: India and Japan elevated their trade and strategic ties with a new Memorandum of Cooperation signed during the 16th Annual Summit in New Delhi. Prime Minister Modi welcomed his Japanese counterpart, highlighting shared priorities for a free and prosperous Indo-Pacific. The leaders discussed initiatives to boost Japanese investment in India, aiming for significant growth in capital and company presence, underscoring a strengthened partnership built on trust and shared values.

title: Air India Express restores Middle East flights: Check routes, destinations and more
content: Air India Express has fully reinstated its Middle East flight operations, bringing back services to Kuwait and Salalah. Flights to Kuwait from Kozhikode and Bengaluru are resuming with increased frequencies. The airline's Middle East network now spans 13 destinations, with approximately 780 weekly flights connecting 18 Indian cities. This restoration marks a significant step in expanding the carrier's regional connectivity.

title: Bears linked to Cardinals pass rusher Josh Sweat in proposed trade
content: The Bears have been linked to Cardinals edge rusher Josh Sweat in a proposed three-team trade that would send Cole Kmet to the Panthers and draft picks to Arizona. Chicago hopes to strengthen its pass rush after recording just 35 sacks last season. Although Josh Sweat posted a career-high 12 sacks in 2025 and remains under a $76 million contract, NFL insider Ian Rapoport has previously said Arizona is not actively shopping him.

title: India return to SL for first Test tour since Kohli's 3-0 sweep; check full schedule
content: The first Test will be played at the Galle International Cricket Stadium in Galle from August 15 to 19. The second Test will take place at the Singhalese Sports Club in Colombo from August 23 to 27.

title: Faulty vacuum cleaner never returned after repair; consumer wins Rs 3,500
content: A Gwalior consumer commission has held Eureka Forbes guilty of poor service after a customer's vacuum cleaner, bought in May 2024, malfunctioned within warranty. Despite multiple repair attempts and the appliance being taken for further fixing in October, it was never returned. The company must now either repair, replace, or refund the Rs 3,699 cost, plus pay Rs 3,500 for mental agony and legal expenses.

title: FPIs continue to exit India: June equity outflows hit Rs 49,340 cr
content: Foreign investors continued their exit from Indian equities in June, withdrawing Rs 49,340 crore amid global risk aversion and domestic valuation concerns. Despite a brief February recovery, outflows have reached Rs 2.7 lakh crore this year, surpassing 2025's total. While equities saw selling, foreign investors remained active in the debt market, with policymakers introducing measures to boost overseas investment.

title: Navy foils piracy bid in Gulf of Aden on cargo ship carrying critical consignment for India
content: Indian Navy warship INS Trikand successfully thwarted a piracy attempt on merchant vessel MV Golden Arsenal in the Gulf of Aden. Responding swiftly to a distress call, the warship's presence caused suspected pirates to flee before they could board. Indian Marine Commandos later secured the vessel, which was carrying vital cargo for India. No crew injuries were reported.

title: 'Issue being overblown': UP dy CM Maurya backs Champat Rai amid Ram Temple row
content: Uttar Pradesh Deputy Chief Minister Keshav Prasad Maurya defended Champat Rai amid embezzlement allegations concerning the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust. While acknowledging the incident's painful nature, Maurya urged against turning it into a public debate, stating investigations are underway. Meanwhile, the Faizabad Bar Association demanded the resignation of Rai and two others, threatening protests if they don't leave Ayodhya within three days.

title: 'Wrote about ending his life': Pilot who hit Beijing's tallest building suffered from anxiety
content: A 66-year-old pilot named Liu faced a harrowing battle with anxiety and suicidal thoughts, culminating in a crash into the CITIC Group headquarters, the tallest building in Beijing. The aircraft, which took off after a routine flight, strayed off its intended path, resulting in injuries to 13 individuals. This alarming incident has ignited serious discussions about aviation security in a city known for its stringent controls.

title: Man legally named 'Santa Claus' arrested in US child predator sting
content: A man legally named Santa Claus, formerly George Quigley, was arrested in Louisiana for allegedly soliciting a minor online. The 75-year-old, who adopted the Christmas persona, was apprehended during a sting operation after engaging in explicit online exchanges and sending nude photos. Police warn parents about the dangers of such online interactions, especially given the suspect's history of posing with children.

title: Why Zelenskyy wants to build US Patriot missiles in Ukraine after Russia's attack
content: In the wake of a substantial Russian airstrike, President Zelenskyy of Ukraine is appealing to the US for permission to manufacture Patriot air defense missiles domestically. This move comes as Kyiv strives to enhance its defenses following attacks that have left 13 dead and over 90 injured. By producing Patriots locally, Ukraine aims to secure a steady supply and lessen its dependence on external aid for sustained security.

title: Yes, we KANE: Harry saves England blushes with shot heard round the World Cup
content: Day 22 saw Harry Kane save England from Congolese chaos with a thunderbolt for the ages, Belgium summon one last Golden Generation miracle from the penalty spot, and the US survive both Bosnia and Herzegovina and VAR’s latest audition for villain of the tournament.

title: 60-year-old man dies after falling into uncovered manhole in Mumbai
content: A 60-year-old man, Aslam Isak Shaikh, has vanished after plunging into an open stormwater drain on Khairani Road in Mumbai's Sakinaka area. The incident occurred while civic workers were undertaking repairs on a drainage grill, leaving a manhole unattended. Authorities are actively conducting search operations and investigating the precise circumstances surrounding Shaikh's disappearance into the drain.

title: Rooftop solar failed to cut bills; consumer wins Rs 25,265 compensation
content: A solar installation firm has been ordered by a consumer commission to refund over Rs 25,000 to a homeowner. The company was found to have overcharged for net-metering and failed to deliver promised electricity generation from a rooftop solar system. The commission ruled the company vicariously liable for its agent's actions and awarded compensation for the shortfall in power and mental agony.

title: 'Eetha': Shraddha Kapoor triples her paycheque to Rs 15 crore for biopic
content: Shraddha Kapoor has reportedly hiked her acting fee to a whopping Rs. 15 crore for her upcoming 2026 film 'Eetha', a biographical drama directed by Laxman Utekar. Based on the life of legendary Marathi Tamasha artist Vithabai Narayangaonkar, Eetha marks a massive salary jump for the actress following the historic success of Stree 2. Backed by producer Dinesh Vijan, the film is scheduled for a theatrical release on August 28, 2026, amid an ongoing title controversy raised by the Nationalist Congress Party (NCP) and Vithabai’s surviving family members.

title: 'Main na thodi funny type ki hoon': Dentist who mocked Ketan's death apologises
content: A dentist, Muskan Soni, has publicly apologized after facing severe backlash for mocking the death of Pune realtor Ketan Agrawal. Soni attributed her actions to a "funny personality" and claimed no intent to offend. The apology follows her five-year suspension from the All India Dental Students and Surgeons Association due to her "inappropriate and disrespectful remarks." Agrawal's death is being investigated as a murder, with two individuals arrested.

title: Sarpanches in Rajasthan, MP linked to trafficking of children
content: An organized child trafficking network has been uncovered in Ahmedabad, with children being recruited from impoverished families in Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh. Village chiefs allegedly act as brokers, facilitating the movement of children for begging at lucrative spots like temples and traffic signals. Authorities have identified 276 trafficked children and are preparing criminal cases against exploiters, aiming to rehabilitate the victims.

title: NET Sociology paper leaves candidates lost in typos
content: Sociology students are voicing their discontent with the latest UGC-NET exam, pointing out critical spelling mistakes, incorrect names of well-known theorists, and awkward translations in Hindi. Errors like confusing 'Ritzer' with 'Putzer' and translating 'social' as 'oval' have raised red flags. Many candidates suspect that certain questions seemed AI-generated, straying from the established syllabus and hindering their understanding during the exam.

title: WhatsApp usernames decoded: Your questions answered, government's concerns explained
content: India's government has urged Meta to halt WhatsApp usernames, citing concerns over potential impersonation and cybercrime. While Meta claims usernames enhance privacy by obscuring phone numbers, officials fear it could aid bad actors. The feature allows users to create unique handles, but the government seeks assurances on its security before widespread adoption. This move could significantly impact how users interact and protect their digital identities.

title: IIT alumnus says AI can't replace the power of the right peer group
content: As AI makes knowledge more accessible than ever, entrepreneur Shikhar Agrawal revisits the value of his IIT Bombay education. He argues that the institution's greatest strength was never its degree or brand name, but the ambitious peer network that shaped his thinking. In the AI era, he believes such transformative learning environments remain difficult to replicate.

title: Kangana Slams Dheeraj Dhoopar: Calls out Shah Rukh claim on Lock Upp; promo sparks fan reactions
content: Kangana Ranaut's return to Lock Upp: Sach Ya Saza has injected fresh drama, with the host sharply critiquing contestants. She notably dismissed Dheeraj Dhoopar's comparisons to Shah Rukh Khan, questioning his 'main character energy.' Kangana also confronted Ram about his approach to the game, advising him against defensiveness. The show, featuring a diverse cast, promises more excitement with Kangana's presence.

title: From grief support to marriage prospects: How AI is transforming life in South Korea
content: South Korea is rapidly integrating AI and robotics into daily life, from recreating deceased loved ones to powering unstaffed businesses. This surge is driven by an aging population and a national strategy to become a global AI leader, with massive investments in chips and robotics. The AI boom is also reshaping careers, making semiconductor engineers highly sought-after and influencing educational choices.

title: 7 killed, 6 injured as boulders cave in at quarry near Bengaluru
content: A devastating rockfall at a quarry in Ramanagara claimed the lives of seven workers and injured at least six others. The tragedy occurred at Cauvery Crushers when loose boulders collapsed on a group of around 20 men. Families of the deceased, who were migrant workers seeking daily wages, are left heartbroken. Authorities have pledged action against illegal quarrying activities.

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ leak case: 1.2 crore people watched Vijay-starrer online, Chennai police tell Madras High Court
content: Justice C. Kumarappan dismisses the bail petitions filed by two of the 21 accused after being told that they have been involved in a serious crime involving online leak of an uncertified movie

title: Raja Raghuvanshi murder: Meghalaya police move Supreme Court against Sonam Raghuvanshi’s bail
content: The Meghalaya High Court, on June 29, had upheld an order of a Shillong trial court granting bail to Ms. Raghuvanshi on the ground that the police had failed to effectively communicate the grounds of her arrest

title: Bihar workers among 8 killed as massive rock collapse hits quarry near Bengaluru
content: At least eight workers were killed after a massive rock collapse at a stone quarry near Bengaluru. Several of the victims were daily-wage labourers from Bihar. Rescue teams are continuing operations while authorities investigate possible safety violations and negligence.

title: Submarine cable system project linking India, Malaysia, Singapore unveiled
content: Microsoft, Lightstorm, Singtel and Tata Communications consortium behind the proposed more than 3,600 km facility

title: Adani Enterprises, UAE’s IHC to invest ₹1.08 lakh crore in Odisha aluminium project
content: The proposed investment is expected to be Odisha’s largest Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) proposal and India’s largest foreign direct investment in the metallurgy sector, government of Odisha officials said

title: Telangana CM Revanth to visit Midjil, where his political journey began two decades ago
content: The visit is being billed not as a routine government programme but as a thanksgiving gesture to the people who first elected him and launched a political journey

title: ‘MAVIGUN’ is YSRCP’s election plank in 2029, says Jagan
content: We will project the proposal as an alternative development model and seek people’s mandate as the Amaravati project has become a conduit for corruption, says the YSRCP president

title: Former AIADMK Minister C. Vijayabaskar, senior leaders join TVK
content: Former Transport Minister M.R. Vijayabhaskar also joins party as exodus from the Dravidian major continues

title: Kerala govt begins examining Adani proposal to sell 49% stake in Vizhinjam port to MSC Group
content: Announcement by Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited has sparked a controversy in Kerala, with Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan claiming Adani Group had not communicated its decision to State govt and that its approval is mandatory for such a move

title: Vikram-1, India's 1st Private Orbital Rocket, Set To Launch By August 4
content: At India's spaceport, the Satish Dhawan Space Centre in Sriharikota, Skyroot Aerospace, the unicorn valued at more than $1.1 billion, has unveiled its fully stacked Vikram-1 launch vehicle.

title: Man Attacks Wife With Axe After Dispute, Then Dies By Suicide In UP
content: Superintendent of Police Vinod Kumar said the incident occurred around 10 am in Rur village under the Khadini police outpost of Saurikh police station.

title: Will Fuel Prices Come Down? Minister Hardeep Puri's "2-3 Months" Hint
content: International crude oil prices have come down, but companies are still processing crude they bought at the height of the West Asia crisis, Minister Hardeep Puri said.

title: Man Sentenced To Death For Murder Of Home Guard On Duty In UP
content: Fast Track Court Judge Ravi Kumar Diwakar convicted Deepak under Section 302 (murder) of the Indian Penal Code and sentenced him to death.

title: No Delay In Test: Cops After Parents Of Thar Hit-And-Run Victim Seek Justice
content: Sarthak Mattoo was killed on June 25 near the Rajokri flyover in southwest Delhi when a Mahindra Thar allegedly struck his motorcycle and drove away.

title: Man Drowns After Falling Into Manhole Amid Heavy Rain In Mumbai
content: The man's umbrella and slippers were found at the spot, and a search operation involving the Mumbai Fire Brigade and the police was launched to trace him.

title: 'Not Siya Point': Locals Say Pune Fort Being Disrespected After Murder Case
content: Locals have demanded that the image of Lohagad Fort, a site with centuries of history and a witness to Shivaji Maharaj's valour, should not be tarnished by such a crime.

title: Weak Monsoon Affects Kharif Crops, Sowing Of Rice, Pulses Sees Sharp Drop
content: According to the Department of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, the total area sown with key kharif crops as of June 25, 2026, has declined by 53.74 lakh hectares, as compared to June 25, 2025.

title: One-Nation-One Election Need Of The Hour, Delhi Will Support: Rekha Gupta
content: Rekha Gupta said the election schedule of Delhi being only one year behind the Lok Sabha elections, adjustments can be made for a national-level synchronization.

title: ISRO Gets Bomb Threat, Mail Sent To Chairman
content: A bomb threat mail was sent to the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) headquarters in Bengaluru. According to police, the threat mail was sent to the chairman of ISRO, Dr V Narayanan.

title: Monsoon Reached J&K A Day Before Delhi. Weather Official Explains Why
content: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) on Wednesday said the southwest monsoon had advanced over the enire Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand regions.

title: Pilot Who Crashed Plane Into Beijing Building Wrote About Suicide In Diary
content: The pilot -- surnamed Liu -- was divorced, lived alone in Beijing and "had long suffered from insomnia and anxiety, and his diary contained multiple references to 'ending his life'", the capital's Chaoyang district government said in a statement.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Settles 579 Points Higher As Crude Oil Eases Further
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty Highlights: Brent crude falls 1.09% to $70.79 while US WTI falls 1.27% to $67.71 per barrel.

title: China's New Ethnic Unity Law Takes Effect Amid Global Rights Criticism
content: China's new ethnic unity law takes effect despite UN, Taiwan, and US criticism over minority rights and its overseas enforcement clause.

title: Hope To Find Survivors Fades As Venezuela Quake Death Count Nears 2,300
content: Venezuela's death toll hits 2,295 with 50,000 still missing a week after twin quakes, as survivors battle food shortages and disease fears

title: Income Tax Return Filing 2026: Six Deadlines Can Save You From Penalties
